product_summary.one_sentence_summary: One-day hands-on AI workshop for a single team that uses the client’s real tasks to build practical productivity workflows.
product_summary.target_user: Team leads in marketing, HR, sales and operations who want to apply AI to real work rather than generic examples.
product_summary.core_pain: Teams want fast productivity gains and implementation help, not just abstract AI education.
product_summary.claimed_outcome: A short workshop should produce ready-to-use workflows, an implementation plan and some early ROI evidence.

primary_buyer_type.reason: The purchase looks like a company-level team enablement decision rather than an individual course purchase.

secondary_buyer_types.reason: HR/L&D often owns employee learning budgets for team workshops.
The buying motion may also involve operations or business-unit management because the workshop uses real team tasks.

budget_owner.reason: Because the session is built around one team’s real work, a business-unit budget is a likely fit.

buyer_influencers.reason: The team manager decides whether the workshop will solve the team’s actual workflow pain.
HR/L&D may influence training format, vendor selection and learning governance.
Innovation or digital-transformation stakeholders may support experimentation with AI workflows.
If the team works with sensitive data, security review can influence whether the workshop is allowed.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: Nobody in Luxembourg offers this format.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: House of Training already offers practical generative-AI and ChatGPT courses in Luxembourg, including training focused on productivity and operational efficiency, so the market is not empty.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: Generic AI examples are the market default.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: The public training pages reviewed are mostly general-purpose or broadly framed, which supports the idea that a real-task customisation angle is a genuine differentiator, even if not unique.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: Luxembourg company training co-funding is relevant to this product category.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: Guichet confirms that Luxembourg businesses can claim co-funding for continuing vocational training, subject to the usual exclusions.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: Practical AI use should be handled with privacy and governance awareness.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: House of Training’s finance AI courses and OpenAI’s prompt guidance both show that practical AI usage is inseparable from safe-use and data-handling considerations.

internal_claims_unverified.claim_text: The workshop should deliver 5-10 ready AI workflows for the team.
internal_claims_unverified.reason_unverified: This is a product-design outcome, not a public fact, and no evidence reviewed proves the number in advance.
The ROI timeline is an internal hypothesis, not an externally verified fact.

internal_claims_unverified.claim_text: SME Package AI 70% applies to this workshop.
internal_claims_unverified.reason_unverified: The reviewed sources do not prove workshop-level eligibility for the exact AI-package claim used in the card.

internal_claims_unverified.claim_text: This is the unique 'work on your tasks' AI format in Luxembourg.
internal_claims_unverified.reason_unverified: The public evidence shows similar practical AI courses already exist, so the uniqueness framing is not reliable.

regulator_entities.name: European Commission AI Office
regulator_entities.relevance: The AI Office publishes AI literacy and implementation materials that shape safe-use expectations for practical AI training.

regulator_entities.name: National market surveillance authorities
regulator_entities.relevance: They enforce Article 4 of the AI Act for organisations using AI systems.

framework_entities.name: AI Act Article 4
framework_entities.relevance: The workshop sits in the same regulatory context as AI literacy and safe AI use.

framework_entities.name: AI Literacy - Questions & Answers
framework_entities.relevance: Official explanation of the AI literacy obligation and its enforcement context.

framework_entities.name: Prompting
framework_entities.relevance: OpenAI’s public guidance on prompt writing provides a free substitute for generic prompting knowledge.

funding_bodies.name: INFPC / Guichet.lu
funding_bodies.relevance: Relevant because the product is a company training format in Luxembourg.

regulatory_requirements.requirement: AI literacy obligations apply to providers and deployers of AI systems.
regulatory_requirements.applies_to: Organisations that deploy AI tools or systems in the EU

subsidies_or_funding.name: Continuing vocational training co-funding
subsidies_or_funding.eligibility_notes: Public co-funding exists for private-sector training plans, but the exact eligibility of this custom workshop must be confirmed.

competitors_or_substitutes.name: House of Training - Generative artificial intelligences - Fundamentals
competitors_or_substitutes.notes: A practical AI fundamentals course that already serves the same productivity-education market.

competitors_or_substitutes.name: House of Training - Entrepreneur augmenté grâce à ChatGPT
competitors_or_substitutes.notes: A hands-on ChatGPT course focused on operational efficiency and prompt engineering.

competitors_or_substitutes.name: OpenAI Academy - Prompting
competitors_or_substitutes.notes: Free public guidance that substitutes for generic prompting knowledge.

legal_claim_severity.reason: This is mainly a productivity workshop, so the formal legal claim is soft, though privacy and governance still matter.

authority_needed_for_delivery.reason: The format can be delivered by an internal trainer, but practical AI use means privacy and safe-use review is advisable.

authority_needed_for_claims.reason: Claims about safe use, workflow reliability and ROI should be checked by an SME or partner, not assumed.

proof_stack.reason: A crowded B2B training market requires proof that one team will actually buy the workshop format.
A paid pilot is the cleanest way to validate the client-specific format.
The workshop should still show that participants can use AI more effectively after the session.
Because the card leans on productivity, measurable workflow or time-saved evidence matters.

proof_burden.reason: Buyer acceptance and measured workflow value matter more than simple attendance in a crowded, substitute-heavy category.

dependency_types.reason: Real client tasks may involve sensitive data or documents, so privacy review is needed.
The workshop is Luxembourg-scoped, so examples, policy and subsidy language should be localised.
An SME reviewer or partner is useful if the workshop claims to improve business workflows.
The sales story depends partly on Luxembourg training co-funding, which should be validated case by case.

scope_variance_risk.reason: The current scope is one-team training, which is coherent, but buyer expectations will vary by department and data sensitivity.

expansion_variance_risk.reason: Moving to other countries or industries changes privacy expectations, AI maturity and subsidy logic materially.

subsidy_eligibility_status.reason: The general training route exists, but the exact 70% AI-package claim for this specific workshop is not proven.

subsidy_conflict_flag.reason: The card presents a strong subsidy story that is not fully confirmed at product level.

market_regulatory_context.reason: The workshop is not regulated itself, but its practical AI usage sits next to AI Act, privacy and governance concerns.

claim_language_risk.reason: The workshop can be positioned safely, but claims about ROI and custom workflow value should stay conservative.

authority_dependency_risk.reason: No formal approval is needed, but privacy and safe-use review matter because the workshop uses real client tasks.

competitive_gap_risk.reason: The market already contains practical AI and ChatGPT training offers, including Luxembourg substitutes.

numeric_claim_risk.reason: The 30-day ROI and workflow-count ideas are internal hypotheses, not public facts.

subsidy_conflict_risk.reason: The card is too certain about a workshop-level subsidy fit that is not yet confirmed.

positioning_risk.reason: The product can easily collapse into a generic AI workshop unless the task-specific customisation story is proven.

claim_conflicts.claim_text: Nobody in Luxembourg offers this format.
claim_conflicts.what_is_wrong: House of Training already sells practical AI courses in Luxembourg that cover generative AI, ChatGPT and productivity use cases.

recommended_manual_review.reason: Prove that real-task customisation is materially different from the existing practical AI training market.
Decide what client-task data can be used safely during the workshop and what must be excluded.
Validate whether the product belongs under general company training co-funding rather than any AI-specific package.

sources.title: Financial aid for in-company continuing vocational training
Entrepreneur augmenté grâce à ChatGPT – Comprendre et intégrer l'IA dans votre stratégie d'entreprise
